title: Flicker Like Fire by Zillick
lyrics: If pain is the price of love, I'd pay it again. Some losses don't echo, they hum, low in the bones, a sound you feel more than hear. And when the world goes quiet, you know, that's when they speak, the loudest. Some losses don't echo, they hum, low in the bones, a sound you feel more than hear. And when the world goes quiet, that's when we speak, the loudest. That's when we speak the loudest. I don't believe in ghosts, but you still talk to me. In the gaps between thoughts and reality, you flicker like fire in a world gone cold. Gone too soon, but never too old. You left with the silence still warm in the room, like breath on glass that fades too soon. I trace your name in the dust on the wall, not to remember, just to feel it at all. I don't believe in ghosts, but you still talk to me. In the gaps between thoughts and reality, you flicker like fire in a world gone cold. Gone too soon, but never too old. I don't believe in ghosts, you flicker like fire in a world gone cold. Gone too soon, but never too old. Time don't heal, it teaches restraint. I carry your shadow like a badge of paint. Some days I laugh, some nights I burn, but I keep walking, I still learn. You were still in my storm, the calm in my scream. Now I build from your memory like bricks in a dream. I'm not okay, but I'm still here. Your voice in my chest keeps the silence clear. Time doesn't heal, it teaches restraint. I carry your shadow like a badge of paint. Some days I laugh, some nights I burn, but I keep walking, I still learn. If pain is the price of love, I'd pay again. You taught me strength, now I lend it to them. We don't get forever, we get a chance. And I'll fight for that with both hands. And I'll fight for that with both hands. You were still in my storm, the truth I defend. And if the story ends here, I'll still write the end. You flicker like fire, but you taught me to burn. So I'll light the path until you return. If pain is the price of love, I'd pay it again. You taught me strength, now I lend it to them. We don't get forever, we get a chance. And I'll fight for that with both hands. You were still in my storm, the truth I defend. And if the story ends here, I'll still write the end. You flicker like fire, but you taught me to burn. So I'll light the path until you return. You flicker like fire, but you taught me to burn. So I'll light the path until you return. You're not gone, just quieter now. But I still hear you, in every step forward. In every flame that refuses to die. I refuse to die. I refuse to die.
